scope_boundary: Only the bounded Project Theseus governance-rights receipt-suite import moves to prototype-backed: a sanitized public-safe fixture records a GREEN local Theseus report with 4/4 passed governance fixtures, 4/4 passed constitutional-predicate fixtures, 4 governance-right records, 4 constitutional-predicate records, 8 evidence-transition records, 8 artifact-graph records, 8 failure-boundary records, zero public training rows, zero external inference calls, and seven expected-invalid controls. This does not promote the Moral Uncertainty and Contestable Governance chapter core claim, the Constitutional Alignment chapter core claim, legal rights, institutional governance, moral correctness, reviewer independence, export usability, safe fork execution, deployed runtime enforcement, clean live Project Theseus replay, safety, alignment, transfer, deployment readiness, or ASI.
transition_reason: The imported Theseus report records a concrete governance-rights receipt suite rather than only a proposed governance vocabulary: audit, redaction appeal, exit, and fork-safety scenarios pass; constitutional-predicate scenarios for least sufficient power, conflict review, constitutional migration, and self-modification weakening are recorded; and the ASI Stack repository validates only a sanitized public-safe summary with overclaim controls. The support movement is therefore limited to this non-core import claim and remains prototype-backed rather than empirical-test-backed or chapter-core evidence.
required_artifacts: sanitized public-safe Theseus governance-rights receipt fixture
negative controls for source hash mismatch, private-payload copying, support promotion, legal-rights overclaim, reviewer-independence overclaim, training-row leakage, and clean-checkout overclaim
negative_results: source report hash mismatch is rejected
public training row leakage is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: the sanitized fixture no longer validates against the recorded source report digest, source checkout boundary, scenario counts, record counts, redaction boundary, or public-safety counts
a source hash mismatch, private payload copy, support promotion, legal-rights overclaim, reviewer-independence overclaim, public training row leak, or clean-checkout overclaim is accepted as valid

future public dependency policy requires archived raw report publication and this record remains a digest-only sanitized summary
promotion_burden: Move beyond this bounded prototype-backed import only after a public-safe archived Theseus report bundle or clean live replay is available, raw-private payload publication boundaries are resolved, governance-right user-facing artifacts can be externally inspected, reviewer independence is recorded, export and fork usability are tested separately, institutional/legal/moral claims are separately justified, residuals and non-claims are preserved, and independent review is recorded with a separate accepted transition.
